characters.name: Anthony Lewis
characters.description: Anthony LewisBiological informationAge17StatusAliveBiographical informationResidenceToledo, OhioAppearancesPortrayed byEddie LeavyDebutCatfish Anthony is a student isJack Griffin's A.P. Bio class atWhitlock High School.  Anthony is often recruited by Mr. Griffin to do some rather controversial tasks, such as stealing the Virgin Mary statue from his own church.  He is also very sarcastic.  He doesn't move very fast, and many of his assigned tasks revolve around his large statue and strength.

characters.name: Ashley
characters.description: AshleyBiological informationGenderFemaleStatusaliveProfessional informationOccupation(s)studentAppearancesPortrayed byAshley TapiaDebutCatfish Ashley (full name Ashley Joy Minette) is a background student who appears in all four seasons. She sits behind Nicholas and in front of Dan. She does not have any dialogue until season 4 episode 1 Tornado where she is depicted as wanting to fit in with the others but is completely ignored by them. In background scenes she is usually paired up with fellow background student Nicholas. As a background student, she has no personality whatsoever, at least none depicted on the show. As a background student, she has no personality whatsoever, at least none depicted on the show.

characters.description: ChloeBiological informationGenderFemaleStatusaliveProfessional informationOccupation(s)studentAppearancesPortrayed byAiesha Eser (S1-2), Unknown actress (S3-4)DebutCatfish Chloe is a background student who does not get a speaking role until Season 2 Episode 1. characters.name: Grace
characters.description: GraceBiological informationAge16StatusAliveBiographical informationResidenceToledo, OhioAppearancesPortrayed bySari ArambuloDebutTeacher Jail Grace(Portayed bySari Arambulo) is a American teenager living in Toledo, Ohio, who attendsWhitlock High Schooland is one of the students assigned toJack Griffin's A.P. BIO class and one of the main teenage characters of the series. She appears in all four seasons of the show and like the other students in the class she takes part in Jack's revenge plans. She works at the local target store, working with in the food section, and is known to advertise Target's deals in class. Grace is depicted as normal in season 1, but starting in season 2 she is depicted as a deranged psychopath.
characters.personality_section: Grace is depicted as normal in season 1, but starting in season 2 she is depicted as a deranged psychopath.

characters.name: Heather
characters.description: HeatherBiological informationAge16StatusAliveBiographical informationResidenceToledo, OhioAppearancesPortrayed byAllisyn SnyderDebutCatfish Heather is a student at Whitlock High School.  Although her design and manner of speaking portray her as a stereotypical nerd, her actions and personality betray that. Heather is a rebellious young girl who is seemingly ready to partake in any manner of delinquent activities and is excited to act out. She is frequently the most loyal of Jack's class. Holds genuine affection for Jack, frequently calling him "chief" or "boss." Among the AP Bio class, her apparent love interest is Dan Decker. - Her overtly sexual messages to Myles turned into a "complex online relationship." - She refers to her father's girlfriend as his "whore," even after her father and the girlfriend get engaged. - She contributes fiction to the Whitlock HS paper regularly---always rejected on the grounds that they cannot publish "mature content." - She had a very brief affair with bull rider Chad Gurn of Prairie High School.

characters.name: Jack Griffin
characters.description: Jack GriffinAliasesMr. GriffinMr. GBoss (Heather)Dr. WhoopsieBiological informationAge"Middle-aged" according toRide the RamGenderMaleStatusAliveProfessional informationOccupation(s)Harvard philosophy professor (formerly)A.P. Biology teacherAffiliationHarvard (formerly)Whitlock High SchoolBiographical informationRelativesRosemary Griffin(mother)John Griffin(father)ResidenceToledo, OhioAppearancesPortrayed byGlenn HowertonTate Birchmore (young)Debut"Catfish" A.P. Biology teacher Whitlock High School Jack Griffin is an intelligent yet egotistical ex- Harvard philosophy professor turned Toledo AP Biology high school teacher. In season 1, he is initially shown to be selfish and is hell-bent on not teaching the students any biology and instead manipulates and uses the students to help him scheme against his rival, Miles Leonard. However, at times he is remorseful for his wrongdoings and sometimes helps the students with their problems. As the seasons progress he is able to forge a closer bond with the students and develop better as a person.

characters.name: John Pugh

characters.name: Lynette
characters.description: Lynette HofstadterBiological informationAge30sGenderFemaleStatusAliveProfessional informationOccupation(s)Payroll accountantAffiliationWhitlock High SchoolBiographical informationResidenceToledoAppearancesPortrayed byElizabeth AlderferDebutWednesday Morning, 8 AM Lynette Marie Hofstadter[1]is a payroll accountant who works atWhitlock High School. She first appears in theSeason 2episode "Wednesday Morning, 8 AM", and serves asJack Griffin's love interest until the first episode ofSeason 4. In "Wednesday Morning, 8 AM", Jack first seeks out Lynette when he discovers that she is in possession of his massage chair. When he requests that she returns it, Lynette quotes philosophy at him and attempts to psychoanalyse Jack's upbringing. While she gets everything wrong and refuses to give up the chair, Jack is instantly smitten by the way they match wits. He schemes with his students to steal the chair back and takes Lynette's chipmunk statue in retaliation. When she returns the chair, he confesses to stealing the chipmunk. In response, Lynette confesses to stealing his wallet off his desk and tells him to keep the chipmunk, as she will "figure out a way to get him back".

characters.name: Marcus Kasperak
characters.description: Marcus KasperakBiological informationGenderMaleStatusActiveProfessional informationOccupation(s)StudentAffiliationWhitlock High SchoolAppearancesPortrayed byNick PeineDebut"Catfish"Marcus Kasperakis a student atWhitlock High School. Marcus is very sarcastic and skeptical of everything Mr. Griffin does, which often lands him in the office or in the back of the room.  He has been conditioned by wealthy parents to believe he is a winner always, and has his heart broken when he finds out he has been lied to. He usually analyses Mr. Griffin's situation perfectly but his analysis is always summarily dismissed by Mr. Griffin.
